---
title: "semiring_ell_rmatvec"
generated: true
description: "Adjoint (transpose) ELL matvec: $Y = A^{ top} X$ for the operand."
---

[â€ą back to `nitrix.semiring.ell`](../nitrix-semiring-ell.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:semiring_ell_rmatvec -->
```python
semiring_ell_rmatvec(values: Num[Array, '... m kmax'], indices: Int[Array, '... m kmax'], X: Num[Array, '... m ncol'], *, semiring: Semiring[Any] = REAL, n_cols: int) -> Num[Array, '... n_cols ncol']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:semiring_ell_rmatvec -->
Adjoint (transpose) ELL matvec: $Y = A^{\top} X$ for the operand.

The transpose companion of [`semiring_ell_matmul`](../nitrix-semiring-ell/semiring_ell_matmul.qmd).  Where the
matmul *gathers* --
$(A X)_i = \sum_p \mathtt{values}_{i, p} \cdot X_{\mathtt{indices}_{i, p}}$
-- this *scatters*:

$$
Y_{c, j} = \sum_{(i, p)\,:\,\mathtt{indices}_{i, p} = c}
    \mathtt{values}_{i, p} \cdot X_{i, j}
$$

for the same implicit $m \times n_{\mathrm{cols}}$ operand
$A$.  Composing the two gives the symmetric-part matvec
$\tfrac{1}{2}(A X + A^{\top} X) = \operatorname{sym}(A)\,X$,
which is what the extremal eigensolvers require when an adjacency was
built by a construction (for example top-k-per-row affinity
sparsification, [`nitrix.sparse.ell_from_dense`](../nitrix-sparse.qmd)) that does **not**
preserve symmetry.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:semiring_ell_rmatvec -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `values` | `Num` | `...` Ă— `m` Ă— `kmax` | â€” | ELL values, shape `(..., m, k_max)`. Pad positions must hold the real additive identity `0`. |
| `indices` | `Int` | `...` Ă— `m` Ă— `kmax` | â€” | ELL column indices, shape `(..., m, k_max)`; the scatter targets. |
| `X` | `Num` | `...` Ă— `m` Ă— `ncol` | â€” | Dense operand indexed by the ELL *row*, shape `(..., m, ncol)`. |
| `semiring` | `Semiring[Any]` | â€” | `REAL` | The algebra to reduce under. **Real semiring only** -- the additive scatter is meaningless for a general monoid (the symmetric part $\tfrac{1}{2}(A + A^{\top})$ needs linear structure, and a non-zero pad identity would scatter spurious mass). Any other algebra raises `NotImplementedError`. The argument is retained for surface parity with [`semiring_ell_matmul`](../nitrix-semiring-ell/semiring_ell_matmul.qmd). |
| `n_cols` | `int` | â€” | â€” | Outer dimension of the implicit operand $A$ and the leading axis of the output. Required: unlike the gather direction it is **not** recoverable from `X` (which is indexed by the row axis `m`). |

**Returns** `Num` â€” shape `...` Ă— `n_cols` Ă— `ncol`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:semiring_ell_rmatvec -->
::: {.callout-note}
JAX-only: there is no Pallas scatter kernel (the matmul backward's
gradient-with-respect-to-`B` scatter is likewise JAX-only), so no
`backend` argument is exposed.  The result is differentiable with
respect to `values` and `X` via the registered real-semiring adjoint
VJP (`indices` is integer-valued and therefore non-differentiable).
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:semiring_ell_rmatvec -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
| `kmax` | *(undocumented â€” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `ncol` | *(undocumented â€” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `n_cols` | *(undocumented â€” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
